body.title: Dev-process artifact memory — the filesystem store
body.summary: One of the TWO kinds of memory in this system: the SOFTWARE-DEVELOPMENT-ARTIFACT store at devlab/runtime/memory/. The sole home for every dev-process artifact — decisions, tickets, slates, sessions, rules, proofs, design_patterns, notes, projects, and these architecture intention-points. One pretty-printed JSON file per record, grep-able and git-backed; searched with `grep -r`. memory_emit.py is the one chokepoint, so a write anywhere else is a detectable error (the path-moves monitor flags it at day-close). Distinct from graph-tree memory: this is the BUILD PROCESS's memory (flat files), not Igor's cognition.

key_concepts.name: categories (folders)
key_concepts.what: decisions/ tickets/ slates/ sessions/ rules/ proofs/ design_patterns/ notes/ projects/ architecture/ — one subdir per artifact type

key_concepts.name: filename convention
key_concepts.what: emitter.namespace.stamp.json; stamp is yyyymmdd.hhmmssuuuuuu (microsecond) so collisions are effectively impossible; same stamp -> idempotent overwrite (updates re-emit reusing the stamp)

key_concepts.name: record envelope
key_concepts.what: {id, emitter, namespace, category, kind, emitted_at, links, body} — the artifact rides in body, links carry semantic-id graph edges

key_concepts.name: canonical-home law
key_concepts.what: D-canonical-memory-consolidation: nothing of these types may live under lab/ or devlab/design_docs[_for_igor]/; the path-moves monitor enforces it

key_concepts.name: projection not relocation
key_concepts.what: migration reads a source and writes a projection, deleting nothing until cutover (SPEC.md migration policy)
